_note: Card meanings, keywords and iconography transcribed verbatim from Yve Lepkowski's own Tarocchino Arlecchino guidebook (Etteilla-system divinatory meanings she assigned). Upright/reversed/keywords are her exact wording; card_desc is a faithful close paraphrase of her iconography text. Preserved here as the provenance record for the grammar.

cards.upright: Illness of the body, disease, physical affliction, infirmity, damage, bad state of health or affairs, disruption, pain, death throes, epidemic, poison, plague, infection.
cards.reversed: Illness of the mind or soul, indisposition, headache, heartache, worry, melancholy, affliction, lethargy, inconvenience, unhappy position, displeasure, anxiety, sorrow, woe, misfortune, medicine, remedy, charlatan, mage, empirical medicine.
cards.card_desc: A jester-like figure in a diamond-patterned costume of red, blue and green, with a pointed bell-tipped hat and black mask with red nose, stands at a table where four small red imp-figures in horned masks watch coins and inverted cups. He holds a striped wand and conceals something in his other hand.

cards.upright: Rest, tranquility, quiet, retirement, withdrawal, private life, secret life, life of solitude, hermitage, religious life, silence, taciturnity, composure.
cards.reversed: Imitation, disguise, image, impersonation, pseudonym, mimicry, parody, camouflage, illusion, reflection, mirroring, replica, simulacrum, counterpart, counterfeit, apocryphal.
cards.card_desc: A harlequin-costumed female pope in a three-tiered crown and veil, black mask with red nose, raises two fingers in benediction while holding crossed red and blue keys. The imagery references the legend of Pope Joan.

cards.upright: Night, darkness, eclipse, mist, obscurity, mystery, secret, hidden, unknown, clandestine, occult, parable, allegory, mystical meaning, mask, veil, cover, sheath, envelop, unvoiced, blindness, oblivion, obfuscate, cover.
cards.reversed: Day, light of day, brightness, clarity, truth, visible, enlighten, manifestation, demonstration, evidence, engender, give life to, uncover, bring light to, publish, come out, bloom, emerge, come to light, zodiac.
cards.card_desc: The Empress wears an alder-leaf crown and holds a globe with a crescent moon and seven-pointed star; her diamond-patterned attire is red, black, pink, yellow, blue and green, with a black-and-gold sun in the upper background.

cards.upright: Support, backing, holding up, column, base, foundation, basis, founding principles, hypothesis, reason, cause, subject, fixity, stability, assurance, persuasion, conviction, confidence, security, reliability, help, aid.
cards.reversed: Protection, defense, influence, benevolence, benefaction, charity, humaneness, goodness, shelter, preservation, aegis.
cards.card_desc: The Emperor in red and black diamond cloaks, a feathered collar and a tall crown holds a small hound and props a spear in his lap. The card illustrates King Herla, the legendary figure whose name connects to Harlequin's etymology and the Wild Hunt.

cards.upright: God, spirit of God, all-powerful, eternal, unique, divinity, devotee, faithfulness, worship, master.
cards.reversed: Philosopher, philosophical, philosophize, sage, wisdom, knowledge, tradition, thought, meditation, contemplation.
cards.card_desc: A robed religious figure holds a red book and a staff topped with a black cross with flaming tips; his hands show stigmata, he wears a three-tiered crown with red veil and a brooch with an inverted cross.

cards.upright: Marriage, wedding, nuptials, alliance, meeting, link, assembly, pledge, promise, oath, intimacy, liaison, junction, conjunction, copulation, coupling, chain, bondage, discomfort, captivity.
cards.reversed: Union, society, contacts, cohabitation, adultery, incest, alloy, blending mixing, amalgam, peace, concord, accord, harmony, coexistence, reconciliation, mending, patching things up.
cards.card_desc: Two Harlequins, one in masculine and one in feminine dress, meet at an ornate fountain crowned by a winged Harlequin drawing a bow at the female figure. Corresponds to Trump VI; in the Etteilla system the Lovers concept was merged with the High Priest card.

cards.upright: Dissension, discord, disturbances, agitation, noise, nuisance, disorder, riots, insurrection, revolt, sedition, rebellion, defection, faction, conspiracy, war, battle, conflict, combat, duel, violence, impulse, outburst, anger, quarrel, dispute, debate, argumentation, struggle, vengeance.
cards.reversed: Arrogance, pride, vanity, false glory, pomp, ostentation, audacity, temerity, presumption, insult, affront.
cards.card_desc: A winged-helmeted Harlequin rides a high chariot, holding a cross-topped globe and a red-bladed sword, drawn by two horses straining in opposite directions with their necks twisted to face each other.

cards.upright: Temperance, moderation, discretion, continence, abstinence, sobriety, frugality, chastity, patience, calm, restraint, accommodation, regard, consideration, mending, mediation, softening, musical tempering, air temperature, climate, temperament.
cards.reversed: Priest, minister, performer of sacrifices, priesthood, clergy, church, council, synod, religion, sect.
cards.card_desc: A seated figure pours liquid between two vessels, wearing a diamond-patterned dress with pink chest armor and gloves, a black mask with green border and a soft white hat.

cards.upright: Justice, equity, probity, uprightness, right, natural right, human right, moral right, public right, civil right, rectitude, reason, execution.
cards.reversed: Jurist, legislator, legislation, tribunal, law, decree, code, order, statutes, precepts, commandment, constitution, domination, institution, natural laws, moral laws, religious laws, civil laws, political laws, laws of war.
cards.card_desc: Justice sits gazing at scales suspended from a globe in her left hand and an upright red sword in her right; black-and-white diamond pattern, pale blue bodice, black mask and a red bicorne hat.

cards.upright: Fortitude, strength, advantage by strength, ability, power, moral strength, greatness of soul, magnanimity, moral or spiritual work, perseverance, constancy, patience, resignation, heroism, courage.

cards.card_desc: A figure in classical garb with a red winged crown embraces a red-striped column with her right arm while gripping a red baton in her left, gazing away from the column.

cards.upright: Fortune, luck, happiness, joy, bliss, improvement, prosperity, bonus, blessing, benefits, graces, favors, goods, wealth, profits, lot, fate, destiny, adventure, good fortune.
cards.reversed: Augmentation, increase, enlargement, abundance, surfeit, growth, development, vegetation, production.
cards.card_desc: A golden wheel with red and green spokes mounted on a twisting green trunk; four Harlequins occupy ascending, crowned-summit, descending and clinging positions on the wheel.

cards.names_en: The Old Man, The Hermit, Time

cards.reversed: Time, kairos, moment, instant, opportunity, occasion, juncture, fleeting, time to act, the right time.
cards.card_desc: A winged Harlequin with a long white beard walks on two crutches, in a patched jacket with long tails, a gold purse at his belt, a tall hexagonal column behind him.

cards.upright: Traitor, deceiver, dissimulation, hypocrite, fanatic, hypocrisy, fanaticism, impostor, disguise, imposture, corruptor, seducer, treason, craftiness, trickery.
cards.reversed: Sacrifice, penance, offering, devotion, renunciation, surrender, loss, abnegation, punishment, correction, penalty, atonement.
cards.card_desc: A Harlequin hangs by one ankle between two ornamental columns, in a black mask with short horns, costume of red, blue, green and brown diamonds with gold puffed sleeves; his tied foot touches a half-red, half-green ceiling.

cards.upright: Mortality, death, passing, end, extinguishing, destruction, attack, murder, assassination, homicide, suicide, regicide, massacre, carnage, butchery, slaughter, occision, poison, deterioration, rot, corruption, putrefaction.

cards.upright: Strength, human strength, extraordinary power, physical strength, virtues, violent impetus, momentum of genius, force of spirit, ravage, violence, vehemence, constraint, firmness, manual or physical labor.
cards.reversed: Lightness, weakness, smallness, tenderness, weakening, dizziness, faint, despondency, depression, languor, collapse, sin, trespass, sacrilege.

cards.names_en: The Lightning, The Tower, The House of God
cards.upright: Destitution, indigence, poverty, scarcity, need, necessity, distress, grief, pain, torment, suffering, affliction, unpleasantness, calamity, adversity, misfortune, bad luck, divine punishment, setback, disgrace, severity, rigidity, harshness.

cards.card_desc: A burning, crumbling diamond-patterned castle; a tower tumbles from the top amid colorful flames erupting through the gate and from two solar discs, two Harlequins fallen in a heap below.

cards.upright: A present, gift, donation, generosity, benefit, good deed, service, homage, tribute, offering, legacy, loyalty, contribution, the color white, precious stone.
cards.reversed: Stripping away, counting, sorting, separation, untangle, inspection, development, deepen, picking-over, remove, deprive, take away, abduct, dispossess, violence, privation, theft, larceny, swindling, fraud, infidelity.

cards.upright: Aim, design, intention, resolution, will, language, words, speech, talk, discuss, babble, chat, gossip, idle talk, reasoning, dialogue, discourse, moon.
cards.reversed: Water, fluid, humid, ablution, dew, rain, deluge, sea, river, torrent, stream, fountain, spring, lake, marsh, flat water, pond, waterfall, waves, inundation, spring.
cards.card_desc: The Moon shows half a pink face behind a green horned crescent mask; two Harlequins below gaze up, the left holding a compass and a starry globe encircled by a gold ouroboros, the right an angled ruler.

cards.upright: Brightening, clearing, explanation, development, instruction, interpretation, analysis, opening, discovery, revelation, clarity, elucidation, light, fire, sun, sky and earth, temple of heat.
cards.reversed: Fire, hot, heat, heat up, cook, heating, spark, ray of light, flow, inflammation, flame, blaze, burn, fires, meteors, lightning, thunderbolt, storm, electricity, St. Elmo's fire, ardor, embrace, fire of love, passion, light up, enflame, internal fire, magnetism, apple of discord.
cards.card_desc: A sun with a red mask, twisting horns and gold-spiral cheeks radiates gold, black, red and green rays; a masked woman sits below on a decorative platform spinning with a distaff.

cards.upright: Voyage, pilgrimage, peregrination, tour, emigration, transmigration, change of scenery, movement, steps, walking, running, rotation, circulation, route, reroute, change course, incursion, flight, escape.
cards.reversed: Earth, matter, cold, thick, mud, sludge, lime, raw material, land, leaf, virgin earth, rural, pastures, orchards, fields, vineyards, terrain, landscape, world, state, realm, countries, regions, nearby, places, site, local, reptiles, quadrupeds, aspect, constancy, stagnation, banks, shore, beach, coasts, reef, rock, plains, mountains, hill, valley.
cards.card_desc: A Harlequin stands atop a globe in a winged helmet and winged slippers, holding a caduceus and a cross-topped globe; the globe's quadrants show clouds, waves, buildings and flames.

cards.upright: Judgement, true judgement, good judgement, sane judgement, fair judgement, discernment, intelligence, reason, understanding, conception, view, thought, comparison, deliberation, opinion, feeling, false judgement, last judgement.
cards.reversed: Judgement, decision, decree, ruling, legal outcome, arbitration, pacification, weak judgement, weak spirit, pusillanimity, injustice, madness.
cards.card_desc: A winged Harlequin blowing a trumpet rises through the air with a halo-like circular hat; below stand three masked Commedia dell'Arte characters — Colombina, Pierrot in white and bearded Pantalon.

cards.upright: Man of the country, country-dweller, rustic, villager, peasant, redneck, farmer, plough-man, grower, agriculture, cultivation, rural, honest man, conscience, integrity, probity.
cards.reversed: Good and severe man, indulgent severity, leniency, indulgence, kindness, tolerance, condescension, 'his bark is worse than his bite.'
cards.card_desc: The King sits at the edge of his throne, in a brown fox-like mask and clothing patterned with apples and pine-bark sleeves, holding a baton topped by a pinecone and resting a hand on a shield reading 'Arlecchino.'

cards.upright: Woman of the country, woman of the woods, craftswoman, thrifty housekeeper, hostess, honest woman, honesty, honor, civility, politeness, good manners.
cards.reversed: Good woman, goodness, excellence, obliging, helpful, good deed, service, charity, alms, favor, obligation.
cards.card_desc: In profile facing left, holding a baton topped with an acorn and an oak-leaf shield, wearing a winged leafed crown, Green Man epaulets and a brown mask with red and green foliage.

cards.upright: Departure, to leave, movement, transfer, distance, remoteness, sending away, absence, abandonment, desertion, change, flight, escape, evasion, transmigration, emigration, transposition, translation, transplantation.
cards.reversed: Disunity, quarrel, rupture, disagreement, squabble, division, part, separation, partition, faction, party, break, cut off, fracture, discontinuity, interruption.
cards.card_desc: A mounted knight holds a thick baton topped with an acorn against his shoulder; both rider and horse wear large feathers, the horse walking right but turning its head left with the knight.

cards.upright: Stranger, foreigner, strange, unusual, rare, uncommon, unaccustomed, unknown, unheard of, incredible, extraordinary, surprising, admirable, marvelous, prodigy, miracle, episode, digression, anonymous.
cards.reversed: News, announcement, notice, warning, admonition, education, instruction, teaching, lesson, view, opinion, notions, anecdotes, chronicle, history, story, tales, fables.
cards.card_desc: A figure stands with hand on hip, holding a baton and looking over his shoulder, in a black mask and tall red leaf-trimmed hat, leaning on a shield with a cross and acorns.

cards.upright: Birth, nativity, origin, creation, source, spring, beginning, principle, primacy, first-fruits, extraction, race, family, house, line of descent, descendant, opportunity, occasion, cause, reason, presage, sign.

cards.card_desc: A green-gloved hand grasps a crowned baton with diamond patterning; the crown bears leaf-shaped points with falling red roses, leaves surround the arm, suns in both upper corners.

cards.upright: Treason, disloyalty, betrayal, infidelity, conspiracy, corruption, malpractice, wrongdoing, crime, subversion, deceit, dissimulation, hypocrisy, duplicity, faithlessness.
cards.reversed: Gold, wealth, opulence, sumptuousness, magnificence, radiance, luster, luxury, abundance, goods.
cards.card_desc: Two sets of four batons arranged diagonally form interlocking X and diamond patterns, two brightly colored batons crossing through the center vertically and horizontally.

cards.upright: Slow-down, delay, putting-back, tardiness, dismissal, suspension, removal, send away, drawing out, lengthening, slowness, reduce speed.

cards.reversed: Wait, awaiting, expectation, expectancy, confidence, foresight, fear, apprehensiveness.

cards.upright: Blond man, probity, equity, integrity, principles, scientist, artist.
cards.reversed: Man of position, man of a high rank, dishonest man, scam, roguery, extortion, pillage, theft, brigand, rascal, corruption, waste, profligacy, squandering.
cards.card_desc: A figure rests a gloved finger on a goblet's hexagonal rim, seated on a throne with one foot raised, in a blue-domed crown with pink stars and a robe with crescent moons and stars over a diamond dress.

cards.upright: Blonde woman, honest woman, virtue, ethics, values, wisdom, honesty, trustworthiness, expert.
cards.reversed: Woman of position, woman of a distinguished rank, dishonest woman, vice, dishonesty, depravity, debauchery, corruption, scandal.
cards.card_desc: The Queen is seated holding a goblet, in a diamond dress of pink, red, white and green with a star-and-crescent cloak fastened by a pale blue heart medallion.

cards.upright: Arrival, coming, approach, advent, agreement, reception, compliance, entrance, get closer, squeeze together, crowd, coming together, meeting, conformity, approximation, accessory.
cards.reversed: Scam, trickery, villainy, fraud, cheating, swindling, ruse, artifice, subtlety, delicacy, nuance, dexterity, suppleness.
